sections.content:
Looking at the image, the black shape moved down. The gray shape is now more visible - it's a vertical bar on the left side of the divider. The black shape's horizontal bar with dots is now below the gray shape's top.
Let me check the segmentation to see if the gray shape's pixel count changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current nodes:")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] in ('B', 'c', 'Y') and node['pixels'] > 5:
        b = node['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {node['color']}: pixels={node['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}], children={node['children']}")

# Check if gray shape pixel count changed
# If DOWN moved the black shape down, maybe the gray shape is no longer being consumed
# Let me try moving UP to see if that helps
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The gray shape still has 18 pixels. The black shape moved down (bbox changed from [15,27]-[23,35] to [18,27]-[26,35]). The gray shape also moved down (from [18,27]-[23,29] to [21,27]-[26,29]).
So both shapes moved down together. The overlap is maintained but the gray shape's pixel count didn't change.
This means DOWN/UP doesn't consume the gray shape, only LEFT/RIGHT does.
Let me go back to the overlap position and try LEFT/RIGHT to consume the gray shape.
Actually, wait. Looking at the segmentation, the gray shape is at [21,27]-[26,29], which is rows 21-26 and cols 27-29. That's 6 rows and 3 columns = 18 pixels. The black shape is at [18,27]-[26,35], which is rows 18-26 and cols 27-35.
The gray shape is within the black shape's column range (27-29 is within 27-35), but the gray shape's rows (21-26) are within the black shape's rows (18-26). So the gray shape is partially within the black shape's bounding box.
But the gray shape still has 18 pixels, meaning it's not being consumed. This suggests that the overlap needs to be more precise.
Let me try moving LEFT to see if the gray shape gets consumed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try LEFT to consume the gray shape
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
